message: Morning, team! Anyone else need a triple espresso today?
You're not the only one, Bez! The reports from last week are still giving me nightmares.
Tell me about it, Tinus.
But hey, at least it's sunny outside today. :sunrise:
True, a little sunshine makes everything better.
Also, I'm having my usual green smoothie. That's my secret energy booster.
Nice, Bez! I'll stick with my coffee though.
Anyone else excited for the launch of the new BitCoin :coin: payment feature this week?
Absolutely, Daniel! Been looking forward to it.
By the way, no spoilers, but did anyone catch the football game? Epic comeback!
I missed it! I was knee deep in a sci-fi novel. Anyone else into that?
Oh, I love sci-fi! Can you share the name of the book, Shay?
I've been reading a bit of sci-fi myself.
Looking forward to sharing recommendations, Shay!
There's always so much happening outside of work! Keep the energy :battery: going, team.
It's going to be another productive day. :raised_hands:

Welcome to The Chain, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

Hey, we're getting reports of customers struggling to make purchases
@here Anyone else seeing this?
Yes, I've noticed the same, @Bob . It seems like an error in the system
Whatever it is, we need to sort it out quickly.
we need to step on it! before the social media backlash is turning into a storm.
Who is handling incidents today? Please size up the issue, this is urgent
Let me see if I can reproduce

I can see the same issue
Daniel Tanya any ideas what that error might mean?
"could not retrieve cart" seems like it could be a cartService issue to me

This is affecting the EU only. This seems to be affecting 62% of users.
Customers are mainly struggling while trying to check out using Bitcoin
:star: Form a working theory
Tanya could you check the logs of the cart service?
Looks like a cart issue
Interesting, i will take a look
SEV-1 Customers unable to select products
*Incident Number INC100079:* SEV-1 Customers unable to select products
:star: Incident record created
:star: Send first comms in first 5 minutes
:star: Qualify the problem
Could it be a change-related issue?
Shay what recent changes have we put out?
Can we have more details on this?
:star: Check changes done to the platform

<https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MjMxNGU0ZjU1ZTVhNDA0NWJmY2M1NjU1OGFiMGY2YjA>
Hope this helps
Bob I can't even select a product, let alone try to checkout with Bitcoin. Are we sure this is connected? Could it be two separate issues?
Bob what time did the customer reports start rolling in?
Looks like cart service is failing due to persistent retries
First reports was submitted around 2 hours ago
I can see an issue with payments

These two changes went out around that time:

• *Change ID:* CHG89778
    ◦ *Owner:* Tanya
    ◦ *Service:* CartRedis
    ◦ *Environment:* PROD
    ◦ *Version:* 4.2
    ◦ *Time Of Change:* 2 hours 46 minutes ago
    ◦ *Description:* Upgrade of CartRedis  to improve cart data persistence and support new payment flows, including Bitcoin transactions.
• *Change ID:* CHG89777
    ◦ *Owner:* Daniel
    ◦ *Service:* CartService
    ◦ *Environment:* PROD
    ◦ *Version:* 3.3
    ◦ *Time Of Change:* 2 hours 53 minutes ago
    ◦ *Description:* Rollout of CartService with updates to cart validation logic and adjustments for Bitcoin-enabled checkouts.
Daniel Tanya could you investigate your two changes and try to work out which could be causing the issue?
I can see that payments are preventing the cart from functioning properly
On it
I suspect that it's my payments change
From our architecture diagram I can't see how an issue with payments would prevent us from selecting products? It seems to be separate from the cart service
I think it's an issue with the front end service calling the carts service
Hamed could you take a look at the logs for the front end service and see what could be causing this?
:star: Manage actions and timings
specifically in relation to the cart service
Sure, I will start investigating
Daniel Tanya are either of those two changes something that we could roll back?
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
I don't see any issues with those deployments
I will continue looking for the cause
Bob are people specifically complaining about not being able to pay with bitcoin, or are any running into the same 500 error that I'm hitting?
Hamed any luck so far?
It looks like the feature implemented for payments is causing all the issues
Still looking
Most of the reports mention issues with checkout or bitcoin
I can see that the cart service failed shortly after Bitcoin change was introduced
The new feature is being called by the frontend and cart
Ah okay, well Daniel is that new payments feature safe to roll back?
:star: Reviewed changes
I will check for dependencies
What's the status of the investigation
Looks like it's probably connected to a change to the payments service, investigating currently
It looks like my change is interlinked with 2 other changes
Can all 3 be safely rolled back?
Checking that now
Checking to see if rolling back is an option
If it looks like danies is refering to cart redis and cart service
I can see that all 3 deployments were pushed around the same time and are relying on each other
You would need to start with cart redis first but it should be fine
Yes, we can rollback all 3 of them without any trouble
Okay, Hamed can you assist Daniel with those rollbacks?
:star: Form an effective investigation team
Sure
Looks like we may need to roll back three changes that are closely linked
Will that have any impact on customers?
What the status?
Working on redis now
Rolling back in progress
Done,  working on cart service
Hamed Daniel are we likely to see any customer impact as a result of this?
rolling back, that is
If I am correct, we should disable the new feature but we should be online
Customer impact is that they wont be able to pay with bitcoin but they will be able to order things
Done
Working on the last change
currently we're sitting at 0 order confirmations so it'll be an improvement
